name: Ojos del Cabriel y cascada del Molino de San Pedro – circular desde Laguna del Marquesado por Montañas Vacías
region_intro: Mountain biking around Laguna Del Marquesado offers routes through a distinctive karstic wetland and mountainous terrain in the Serranía de Cuenca. The landscape features limestone formations, significant elevation changes, and extensive pine forests. Situated at over 1,300 meters of altitude, the area provides a truly mountainous setting with varied terrain for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Laguna Del Marquesado The most popular mountain bike trail is Mountain biking in the Alto Tajo Natural Park, a 47.6 miles (76.7 km) difficult route that takes 6 hours 22 minutes to complete. This trail explores the diverse landscapes of the Alto Tajo Natural Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ojos del Cabriel, Herrería and San Pedro Waterfalls Loop, a moderate 16.3 miles (26.2 km) path. This route features scenic views around the Ojos del Cabriel and local waterfalls. Local mountain bikers also love the Nacimiento del río Tajo – circular desde Valdemeca por el Parque Natural Serranía de Cuenca, a 30.5 miles (49.2 km) trail leading through the Parque Natural Serranía de Cuenca, often completed in about 4 hours 45 minutes. Mountain biking around Laguna Del Marquesado is defined by karstic wetlands, elevated mountainous landscapes, and lush pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Laguna Del Marquesado are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from several reviews. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Laguna Del Marquesado's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Laguna Del Marquesado?
A: There are over 8 mountain bike routes available in the Laguna Del Marquesado area, offering a range of experiences through its unique karstic wetland and mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking in Laguna Del Marquesado?
A: You can expect a diverse and challenging landscape. The area features a distinctive karstic wetland, limestone formations, and extensive pine forests. Situated at over 1,300 meters of altitude, the terrain is mountainous with noticeable elevation changes, providing varied surfaces from flowing dirt paths to potentially rockier sections.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for mountain bike trails in this area?
A: Mountain biking in Laguna Del Marquesado offers routes primarily in the moderate to difficult range. There are 2 moderate trails and 6 difficult trails, ensuring options for experienced riders seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that feature waterfalls or rivers?
A: Yes, several routes highlight the area's beautiful water features. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003543651/ojos-del-cabriel-herreria-and-san-pedro-waterfalls-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel, Herrería and San Pedro Waterfalls Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that takes you past the scenic Ojos del Cabriel and local waterfalls. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017162621/ojos-del-cabriel-y-cascada-del-molino-de-san-pedro-circular-desde-laguna-del-marquesado-por-montanas-vacias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel y cascada del Molino de San Pedro – circular desde Laguna del Marquesado por Montañas Vacías&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult route that also includes these natural attractions.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Laguna Del Marquesado?
A: The area is renowned for its scenic beauty, which is particularly intense during spring and after periods of rain when water levels rise. These times offer lush landscapes and vibrant natural surroundings. Early mornings and late afternoons (dawn and dusk) are also recommended for the best visual appeal.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often pass by significant natural features. You can encounter the unique karstic wetland of the Laguna del Marquesado itself, as well as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrería Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/604533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro Mill Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4547251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4785768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Marquesado Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt; is also a key highlight.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, the area offers several difficult and longer routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1494760598/mountain-biking-in-the-alto-tajo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountain biking in the Alto Tajo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 76.7 km route with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38141718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huélamo – Spring and washhouse loop from Molatilla del Molino&lt;/a&gt;, which spans nearly 97.5 km.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Laguna Del Marquesado?
A: The routes in Laguna Del Marquesado are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, the unique karstic wetland, and the extensive pine forests that define the mountainous setting.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003543651/ojos-del-cabriel-herreria-and-san-pedro-waterfalls-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel, Herrería and San Pedro Waterfalls Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Another notable circular trail is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016891197/nacimiento-del-rio-tajo-circular-desde-valdemeca-por-el-parque-natural-serrania-de-cuenca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nacimiento del río Tajo – circular desde Valdemeca por el Parque Natural Serranía de Cuenca&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 49.2 km route.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I spot while mountain biking in Laguna Del Marquesado?
A: The lagoon and its surroundings are a natural refuge for diverse flora and fauna. Birdwatchers might spot species such as the Woodcock, Cascade Sandpiper, Dipper, little grebe, mallard, moorhen, and common coot. The wider reserve is also home to various other birds, amphibians, reptiles, and mammals, including otters, foxes, and wildcats.

Q: Is there a specific route that explores the Parque Natural Serranía de Cuenca?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016891197/nacimiento-del-rio-tajo-circular-desde-valdemeca-por-el-parque-natural-serrania-de-cuenca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nacimiento del río Tajo – circular desde Valdemeca por el Parque Natural Serranía de Cuenca&lt;/a&gt; is a 49.2 km difficult trail that leads directly through this natural park, offering an immersive experience of its landscapes.
tips: This section is before reaching Zafrilla and before and after it. I confess that the slope is tough and, especially if you do it on a hot day, you will have to take refuge (as was my case) in the shadows of the few scrubland trees to cope with the difficulty.

Church of the town of El Vallecillo, a very quiet place with few inhabitants, one more example of the uninhabited area. From here several trails lead to the Cascada del Molino de San Pedro and the Ojos del Cabriel

The water springs that supply the river, in this case the Cabriel, are known as Ojos. The water springs from the ground and in this area there are two of them very close. It is reached by a very entertaining path without technical difficulty.

** The water springs that supply the river, in this case the Cabriel, are known as Ojos. The water springs from the ground and in this area there are two of them very close. It is reached by a very entertaining path without technical difficulty

The Cascada de la Herrería is a natural gem located in the Sierra de Albarracín, near the town of El Vallecillo. This beautiful waterfall, also known as the “Hidden Waterfall,” has a drop of approximately 8 meters over the Cabriel River. 
To reach the waterfall, you can follow a hiking route that begins in El Vallecillo. The route is short, just half a kilometer, and takes you through a varied and picturesque landscape. During the walk, you will also be able to see the natural springs known as the Ojos del Cabriel, which feed the river and the waterfall.

Without a doubt one of the most beautiful waterfalls with a pool I have been to in Spain. Its location, in what is called "emptied Spain" due to its low population density, has prevented its overcrowding, although in summer there were quite a few people bathing. It is advisable to wear swimming or canyoning shoes to enter and exit the water through the porous stones. The view from the cavity behind the waterfall is simply stunning.

waypoints.name: Church of the Holy Trinity (El Vallecillo)
